response:
WHEN MOORE'S LAW WILL STOP APPLYING DEPENDS ON THE PHYSICAL LIMITATIONS OF SEMICONDUCTOR TECHNOLOGY. AS TRANSISTORS GET SMALLER, THEY APPROACH THE POINT WHERE QUANTUM EFFECTS AND HEAT DISPERION MAKE FURTHER MINIATURIZATION UNFEASIBLE. MOST EXPERTS PREDICT THAT THIS LIMIT WILL BE REACHED WITHIN THE NEXT 15 TO 20 YEARS, ALTHOUGH NEW MATERIALS AND ARCHITECTURES MAY EXTEND ITS LIFE.

***

THE END OF MOORE'S LAW WILL NOT MEAN THE END OF INNOVATION IN COMPUTING, BUT IT WILL FORCE THE INDUSTRY TO EXPLORE ALTERNATIVE APPROACHES LIKE QUANTUM COMPUTING, NEUROMORPHIC ENGINEERING, AND SPECIALIZED HARDWARE. THIS TRANSITION WILL REQUIRE SIGNIFICANT INVESTMENT IN RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT TO MAINTAIN THE RATE OF PROGRESS IN COMPUTING POWER AND EFFICIENCY.

THE END
